steps.title: Determine the time interval from the exponent structure
steps.content: The exponent is $2x$ where $x$ is measured in years. The coefficient 2 means that for every 1 year that passes, the exponent increases by 2. Equivalently, for every $0.5$ year (6 months) that passes, the exponent increases by 1.

steps.title: Calculate the growth factor for a 6-month interval
steps.content:
When 6 months pass, $x$ increases by $0.5$ years, so the exponent becomes $2(0.5) = 1$. The growth factor is therefore $(1.005)^1 = 1.005$.

Interpreting this result:
$$1.005 = 1 + 0.005 = 1 + 0.5\%$$
So the balance is multiplied by 1.005 each 6-month period, representing a 0.5% increase from the balance at the start of that interval.

steps.title: Match to the correct choice
steps.content:
We determined that every 6 months, the balance is multiplied by a factor representing a 0.5% increase from the beginning balance of that interval.

Choice C — At the end of every 6-month interval, the balance increases by about 0.5% of the balance at the beginning of the 6-month interval. states exactly this: At the end of every 6-month interval, the balance increases by about 0.5% of the balance at the beginning of the 6-month interval. That matches Choice C — At the end of every 6-month interval, the balance increases by about 0.5% of the balance at the beginning of the 6-month interval..
